cultures.name: The Seyric Horizon
cultures.historicalSummary: the earliest secure sealing horizon, known from funerary closures, threshold wards, and stone stoppers. Its self-name is unknown; “Seyric” is the modern archaeological exonym.

cultures.name: The Myratic Tradition
cultures.historicalSummary: the isolated Myrat complex, whose deliberate hollows may stand for complete seal-patterns. No direct Seyric lineage is established. Supplying a pattern is its first technique, not the identity of the whole tradition.

provenance.summary: a basalt stopper recovered in place from Ossuary I at Seyr, the earliest securely excavated intact closure assigned to the horizon.
provenance.function: contained a simple mortuary curse within the burial niche.

provenance.summary: the fourth slate closure catalogued from the outer cairn cache at Seyr; tool marks suggest it was cut during one workshop episode.
provenance.function: reinforced a cache curse through repeated nested closure.

provenance.summary: a broken limestone lintel ward from the Orra Gate. The forked interior appears to be a later repair or accretion rather than the original carving.
provenance.function: confined a threshold curse while allowing one obstructing fragment to be cleared during licensed passage.

provenance.summary: the eighth closure recorded in the Tel Vey storage chamber; its repeated inner fragment remains unusually crisp beneath mineral deposits.
provenance.function: reinforced a chamber curse by repeating an older surrounding form inside a deeper field.

provenance.summary: an unpigmented steatite practice fragment from the Seyr workshop refuse; its central ring encloses no mark and was cut deliberately.
provenance.function: isolated terminal removal of an empty ownership ring between a familiar pair of veils.

provenance.summary: a bronze-faced reliquary closure from Auten, preserving the first securely dated Seyric ring with a bound colored mark. Its discovery forced a revision of the horizon's chronology.
provenance.function: returned a marked condition through a veiled chamber while keeping both occurrences under one ring.

provenance.summary: A two-pigment Seyric exercise places distinct globally bound marks beneath nested ownership rings.
provenance.function: Trained readers to follow each occurrence to its actual owner rather than the nearest visible boundary.

provenance.summary: A distributive Seyric tablet contrasts a negative A-and-X source field with its positive A continuation target.
provenance.function: Required erasing X only from the legal negative field before the copied A branch can support both continuations.

provenance.summary: A compact Seyric workshop tablet where removing a supported negative-P echo exposes Q for a separate Q-to-R continuation.
provenance.function: Use exact marked-fragment deiteration as the causal first step of a continuing propositional route.

provenance.summary: A Seyric tablet with a retained R field, a shallow marked negative P source, and a blocked target requiring P together with Q.
provenance.function: Required insertion of Q into the exact shallow source before the completed source can discharge the blocked target.

provenance.summary: A Seyric distribution tablet carries the intact implication from P-and-S to Q-and-R into two descendant result branches.
provenance.function: Required copying the complete cut-delimited implication; its atoms and inner regions have no independent copy authority.

provenance.summary: The propositional tablet `(R ∧ (P ∨ (Q ∧ R))) → (P ∨ Q)` guards one atomic P occurrence inside a larger continuation.
provenance.function: Required wrapping exactly P so the resulting double-cut occurrence can feed the guarded P-and-Q continuation.

provenance.summary: an unprovenanced alabaster votive from Myrat whose repeated same-owner field marks accompany a separately incised paired closure unlike the Seyr teaching tablets.
provenance.function: tested whether duplication and flat-group recognition transferred to an unfamiliar material and two-obligation composition.

provenance.summary: The direct closed theorem (A∧B)∨(A∧C) → A∧(B∨C) presents the shared A inside both source alternatives and asks for one factored A with the residual B/C choice preserved.
provenance.function: The common conjunction component must be recovered from both alternatives while the residual alternatives remain grouped in the conclusion.

provenance.summary: (A∨B)∧(A∨C) exposes atomic A as the exact common alternative to factor into A∨(B∧C).
provenance.function: A must be matched as the common alternative in both clauses; no separately available A can bypass the factoring relation.

provenance.summary: The whole common alternative K=(P∨Q) occurs in both clauses ((K∨R)∧(K∨S)) and must remain intact in K∨(R∧S).
provenance.function: Matching only P, only Q, or the silhouette of a partial alternative does not establish equality of the complete common disjunction.

provenance.summary: A P-and-Q support faces a deeper P-Q-P field whose exact wrapped subgroup must remain recognizable.
provenance.function: Wrapping intact P-and-Q creates a matching inner cut that can be discharged; wrapping P alone or the larger P-Q-P field cannot.

provenance.summary: Eligible and obstructed P-and-Q double cuts coexist in one workspace beside a P context and an exact P-P-Q support.
provenance.function: Eliminating the empty-annulus pair preserves its P and Q beside the context for a later exact discharge; the annulus-owned blocker prevents the near-match from making that transition.

provenance.summary: The direct closed theorem A∨(B∧C) → (A∨B)∧(A∨C) asks the same A alternative to survive in both conjunctive conclusions while B and C remain distinct.
provenance.function: The shared alternative must participate in both conjunctive outputs, not only one.

provenance.summary: a three-owner basalt proof tablet showing one common condition carried into two alternative clauses.
provenance.function: introduced the canonical expansion of a conjunction over a disjunction in Seyric ward accounting.

name.curatorShorthand: rings do not turn the field

provenance.summary: A didactic slate carrying four identical marks from one ownership ring through a three-veil alternating chain.
provenance.function: trained ward-readers to derive each mark's host polarity from the complete veil path while treating the ownership ring as parity-neutral.

provenance.summary: A gypsum seal whose asymmetric R and S-and-T branches share the intact compound P-or-Q before feeding U.
provenance.function: Factor the common compound P∨Q and consume the factored result through a downstream U continuation.

provenance.summary: The theorem `[P ∧ (P→(Q∧S)) ∧ (Q→R)]→R` joins extraction of Q from an intermediate product to a later implication.
provenance.function: Required carrying P into the compound Q-and-S result, extracting Q, and continuing through Q-to-R.

provenance.summary: a steatite apprentice tablet from the Seyr workshop refuse lens, incised with one ring and a deliberately repeated pair of matching field marks.
provenance.function: trained seal-cutters to recognize that repeating an identical condition within one field does not create a second obligation.

provenance.summary: A Seyric propositional tablet whose visible arrangement isolates compound weakening boundary.
provenance.function: A complete compound proposition must remain intact while a separate irrelevant compound semantic subgraph is erased as one whole fragment.

provenance.summary: A later Seyric tablet juxtaposing one tiny sufficient veil with the densely nested implication (P∧Q)→(R∨S).
provenance.function: Trained readers to recognize and erase one large irrelevant proposition as a complete fragment instead of working through its internal cuts and marks.

provenance.summary: a small repetitive tablet from Seyr workshop refuse, probably a routine exercise or production trial rather than a commissioned closure.
provenance.function: practiced separating two nested owners while retaining only the mark required by the seal.

provenance.summary: A Seyric propositional tablet whose visible arrangement isolates compound projection.
provenance.function: A grouped conjunct must be projected intact as one proposition instead of selecting one of its internal atoms.

provenance.summary: A Seyric propositional tablet whose visible arrangement isolates ternary projection choice.
provenance.function: Exactly three product components offer distinct targets and only one feeds the goal.

provenance.summary: A fine-grained clay teaching seal whose first pigment recurs in the left member of a paired alternative enclosure.
provenance.function: Taught apprentices to certify an alternative seal from a premise matching its left branch.

provenance.summary: A four-pigment workshop tablet with an R-and-not-T context and a shallow T source missing one boundary-delimited not-(P-and-Q) fragment.
provenance.function: Required insertion of the intact cut-delimited group so the completed source could discharge the exact blocked target.

provenance.summary: a limestone gate seal from Orra with two differently owned alternatives, one of which repeats the single marked condition admitted outside the branch field.
provenance.function: tested whether ward readers chose the supported alternative by ownership rather than applying a fixed left-branch habit.

provenance.summary: A Seyric propositional tablet whose visible arrangement isolates compound conjunction idempotence.
provenance.function: The same nontrivial compound proposition repeats whole across a conjunction, while partial internal matches do not count.

provenance.summary: a fine limestone chamber seal from Tel Vey whose two sibling veils preserve the same pigment and ring ownership under separate cuts.
provenance.function: demonstrated that repeating one permitted alternative in two branches does not widen the ward's licensed passage.

provenance.summary: A Seyric propositional tablet whose visible arrangement isolates compound disjunction idempotence.
provenance.function: The same nontrivial compound proposition repeats whole across a disjunction, while partial internal matches do not count.

provenance.summary: A Seyric propositional tablet whose visible arrangement isolates atomic conjunction exchange.
provenance.function: Two atomic conjunction operands are exchanged while remaining under the same semantic owner.

provenance.summary: A Seyric propositional tablet whose visible arrangement isolates compound conjunction exchange.
provenance.function: A compound conjunction operand is exchanged whole with another operand while every semantic owner remains fixed.

provenance.summary: a matched limestone lintel fragment from the Orra Gate whose paired alternatives recur in opposite carved positions on the inner and outer faces.
provenance.function: showed ward readers that the two licensed passages remained the same alternatives regardless of which branch the carver placed first.

provenance.summary: A source disjunction contains P and the intact compound branch Q∧R. The target shows those branches exchanged inside a deeper field beside unrelated X.
provenance.function: Wrapping exactly the two exchanged branches creates their missing disjunction boundary; either branch alone or including X produces the wrong structure.

provenance.summary: a broad slate workshop tablet from Seyr bearing three differently pigmented ring marks in a single uninterrupted field, copied in several spatial clusters by apprentice hands.
provenance.function: trained readers to treat every apparent clustering of three joint conditions as one field unless an actual boundary separated them.

provenance.summary: A flat P, Q, and R-or-S product must be recognized as one three-role source for a T-to-U continuation.
provenance.function: Choose the full conjunction scope while preserving the opposite-connective R∨S role intact.

provenance.summary: a chamber closure from Tel Vey bearing three sibling branch cuts under one outer field; later copies cluster the branches differently without adding inner boundaries.
provenance.function: trained ward readers to preserve the same three licensed alternatives across differing scribal parenthesizations.

provenance.summary: A Seyric routing tablet whose intact marked P-and-Q fragment feeds a two-link continuation through R to S.
provenance.function: Required recognition and preservation of the complete marked source while routing it through both downstream implications.

provenance.summary: A Seyric propositional tablet whose visible arrangement isolates disjunction reassociation role scope.
provenance.function: Among multiple disjunction sites, one visibly compound opposite-connective proposition must remain one semantic role, and that role identifies the correct scope boundary.

provenance.summary: An early connective-period slate arranging three owned pigments across two implication links and a final return veil.
provenance.function: Introduced the workshop method for carrying a starting condition through a three-proposition implication chain.

provenance.summary: a long slate exercise tablet from Seyr with four pigment owners and three evenly spaced implication chambers.
provenance.function: trained advanced readers to carry one licensed condition through a three-link sequence without confusing the intermediate owners.

provenance.summary: a weathered slate retrieval tablet combining the classic three-proposition chain with a separate paired closure.
provenance.function: The compound premise P∧S composes through the compound handoff Q∧T to R.

provenance.summary: a bronze-faced calculation tablet from Auten whose first implication chamber admits two owned marks while only one advances into the second chamber.
provenance.function: trained readers to preserve a standing condition alongside a changing implication chain.

provenance.summary: a limestone teaching seal from Tel Vey with two parallel implication chambers feeding one paired terminal field.
provenance.function: trained readers to transform two independent conditions and retain both results as one joint obligation.

provenance.summary: Lift independent compound-output implications together into one four-owner conjunction
provenance.function: Lift independent compound-output implications together into one four-owner conjunction

provenance.summary: a limestone lintel from Orra with two parallel conditional wards, a forked alternative field, and one shared terminal mark.
provenance.function: required gate readers to verify that either licensed approach led to the same protected chamber.

provenance.summary: a compact Veyr correction slate preserving three nested alternatives around one shared terminal condition.
provenance.function: Resolve separate P, Q, and R implications to shared compound S∧T

provenance.summary: an alabaster teaching tablet from Myrat with one conditional pigment change repeated across only one arm of a forked field.
provenance.function: trained readers to transform one licensed alternative while carrying the other through unchanged.

provenance.summary: Map a compound P∧T branch to Q∧U while preserving compound R∧S
provenance.function: Map a compound P∧T branch to Q∧U while preserving compound R∧S

provenance.summary: P∨Q∨R is paired with P→S, Q→T, and R→U, requiring three corresponding output alternatives S∨T∨U.
provenance.function: Binary position guessing does not generalize: each of three source branches must be matched to its own result by correspondence.

provenance.summary: A two-pigment slate placing a standalone A beside an A/B alternative enclosure and a single A return.
provenance.function: Introduced conjunction absorption by showing that required A already satisfies the alternative containing A.

provenance.summary: a bronze exercise tablet from Auten placing a plain P beside a forked P-or-Q field and repeating P as the terminal condition.
provenance.function: Absorb (P∨Q)∧((P∨Q)∨(R∧S)) to the compound repeated proposition P∨Q.

provenance.summary: a limestone gate lintel from Orra with one plain P branch and one P-and-Q branch converging on the same terminal ownership mark.
provenance.function: trained readers to recognize that an extra condition on one route did not change the condition common to both licensed routes.

provenance.summary: Absorb a compound repeated P∧Q from (P∧Q)∨((P∧Q)∧R)
provenance.function: Absorb a compound repeated P∧Q from (P∧Q)∨((P∧Q)∧R)

provenance.summary: a two-owner basalt theorem tablet setting an implication beside its polarity-reversed counterpart.
provenance.function: introduced direct contraposition to classical ward readers without invoking a separate citation.

name.professional: The Tel Vey Contraposition Recall Plaque

provenance.summary: an unannotated fired-clay plaque from Tel Vey repeating the classical two-owner theorem after delayed study.
provenance.function: Direct contraposition with a conjunctive antecedent

provenance.summary: A single proposition and its veiled complement form an exhaustive split.
provenance.function: Atomic excluded middle

provenance.summary: A classical-period slate placing one pigment and its veiled complement in a single alternative enclosure.
provenance.function: Excluded middle for a two-owner conjunction

provenance.summary: A contrast slate recutting the complementary branches with stronger polarity marks.
provenance.function: Excluded middle for a three-owner conjunction

name.professional: Seyric Atomic Negated Disjunction Forward Tablet

provenance.summary: A negated disjunction whose two operands are atomic expands into the conjunction of their negations, with no compound operand obscuring the base direction.
provenance.function: de-morgan-negated-disjunction-forward

name.professional: Seyric Atomic Negated Disjunction Reverse Tablet

provenance.summary: A conjunction of two negated atomic operands factors into their negated disjunction, with no compound operand obscuring the base reverse direction.
provenance.function: de-morgan-negated-disjunction-reverse

provenance.summary: A two-pigment classical slate juxtaposing a veiled conjunction with two separately veiled alternatives.
provenance.function: Expand negated conjunction with atomic operands

name.professional: Seyric De Morgan Polarity Contrast K-06

provenance.summary: A three-pigment contrast tablet opposing one veil around a product to three separately veiled alternatives.
provenance.function: Expand negated conjunction across three peer operands

name.professional: Auten De Morgan Bifurcation Tablet ED-I

provenance.summary: A silver-black tablet whose denied fork opens into one intact compound refusal and one atomic refusal, each feeding a separate continuation.
provenance.function: Expand negated disjunction with conjunctive left operand

name.professional: Auten De Morgan Convergence Tablet FC-I

provenance.summary: a blackened bronze tablet whose two denied branches converge beneath one conjunctive seal.
provenance.function: Factor negated conjunction with atomic operands

name.professional: Seyric Compound Conjunction De Morgan Tablet

provenance.summary: A conjunction-family De Morgan tablet preserves one disjunctive component as a whole while transforming it beside an atomic peer.
provenance.function: de-morgan-compound-conjunction-family

provenance.summary: A crowded painted limestone seal placing one direct P/not-P complement beside a coherent six-region transitive chain.
provenance.function: Trained recognition that an intimidating but irrelevant connected fragment can be removed whole before resolving the simple complement.

provenance.summary: a two-owner basalt theorem tablet whose P trace returns through a nested implication loop.
provenance.function: Peirce feedback ((P implies (Q and R)) implies P) implies P with an explicit compound feedback endpoint

provenance.summary: a painted limestone variant marking the returning P path separately from the forward P-to-Q implication.
provenance.function: Peirce feedback with a conjunctive returned proposition P-and-R

provenance.summary: P, the required whole context S∧T, and the near-match S∧U sit beside a P∧(S∧T) to Q∧(S∧T) to R∧(S∧T) chain.
provenance.function: Shared atoms are not enough: the complete S∧T context, rather than the similar S∧U context, is the invariant consumed at both handoffs.

name.professional: Seyric Assumption Relevant Structured Reductio Tablet

provenance.summary: Under denial of a compound conclusion, one structured contradiction depends on that denial while a competing contradiction is visible but cannot discharge it.
provenance.function: reductio-assumption-relevant-contradiction, mixed-structured-contradiction-reductio

provenance.summary: A six-pigment slate where weakening P-and-Q-and-R creates the exact P-and-Q source for one branch of an alternative consumer leading through T to U.
provenance.function: Combine whole-source weakening with useful alternative placement and an asymmetric downstream continuation.

provenance.summary: One supplied implication becomes the exact missing middle bridge only after contrapositive reversal, enabling composition with a second link.
provenance.function: mixed-contrapositive-bridge

name.professional: Seyric Excluded Middle Manufactures Cases Tablet

provenance.summary: No split is supplied; downstream handlers for a proposition and its negation identify the useful excluded-middle proposition, and both manufactured cases are consumed.
provenance.function: mixed-excluded-middle-manufactures-cases

provenance.summary: An alternating connective boundary requires both De Morgan and distribution to produce a recognizably CNF-shaped intermediate consumed downstream.
provenance.function: mixed-alternating-negation-cnf

provenance.summary: An alternating connective boundary requires both De Morgan and distribution to produce a recognizably DNF-shaped intermediate consumed downstream.
provenance.function: mixed-alternating-negation-dnf

provenance.summary: a dense slate weave from Veyr preserving one side premise across a composed implication route and a two-case marginal diagram.
provenance.function: Connected branch map into a side-bearing two-case chain

provenance.summary: P and Q prepare locally to R and S, converge separately to T, and only then share the single T→U continuation.
provenance.function: The common tail cannot be applied before the branch-specific preparations establish its shared T input.

provenance.summary: The P branch follows P→R→T while the Q branch follows Q→S→T before the cases converge.
provenance.function: A single shared implication cannot stand in for the two different local intermediate states.

name.professional: Seyric Classical Consensus Branch Building Tablet

provenance.summary: A manufactured P/not-P split builds two branches that must both preserve and consume the same shared factor.
provenance.function: Required coordinating excluded-middle branch construction with a shared consensus factor across the branch boundary.

name.professional: Seyric Classical Consensus Product Collapsing Tablet

provenance.summary: A classical split and dual distribution expose redundant product structure whose shared term changes the available collapse.
provenance.function: mixed-classical-consensus-product-collapsing

provenance.summary: Releasing the outer double negation turns ¬(P∨Q) into the full product ¬P∧¬Q; the ¬P conjunct is then projected as the exact antecedent consumed by ¬P→R.
provenance.function: Recognizing De Morgan is not the endpoint: the produced connective is a product, and one exact product component must feed the later implication consumer while the other remains visible context.

provenance.summary: A De Morgan split sends ¬P and ¬Q through separate case handlers to R, then requires the shared R→S continuation against a denied S.
provenance.function: Required treating the produced alternatives as cases and carrying their common R result through a causally necessary downstream continuation.

provenance.summary: A cut-delimited P source and a P inside an incomparable deeper field begin without a usable copy direction.
provenance.function: Wrapping exactly the deeper P creates a descendant cut matching the matrix-level source; wrapping Q or P+Q produces the wrong structure.

provenance.summary: A complete P-Q double cut sits in a negative matrix whose existing positive cuts are inside or beside it rather than useful ancestors.
provenance.function: Wrapping the complete target creates a positive ancestor annulus where inserting the exact P-Q double cut enables its discharge; a partial shape or the wrong host does not.

provenance.summary: One erasable-looking P source supplies both P→Q and P→R branches before Q and R jointly meet a denied conjunction.
provenance.function: Required preserving the sole ancestor source through two separate downstream uses; removing it disables both branches.

name.professional: Tel Vey Common-Factor Recall Seal R-4

provenance.summary: a limestone recall seal placing an absorbed conjunction beside an unrelated closure pair.
provenance.function: Factor compound P∧Q from ((P∧Q)∧(P∧Q))∨((P∧Q)∧(R∨S)); the result is (P∧Q)∧((P∧Q)∨(R∨S)), which absorbs to P∧Q.

provenance.summary: a weathered gate lintel combining a three-way case field with a separate closure pair.
provenance.function: Three cases produce two clauses sharing alternative S; factoring them yields S∨(S∧U), which is then absorbed to S.

provenance.summary: The whole context K=U∧V is supplied outside P∨Q, the branches map to R and S, and K must then be recollected with each mapped result by the downstream consumers.
provenance.function: Mapping the branches does not finish their preparation; the exact whole shared K must be matched again after the branch-local result appears.

provenance.summary: an alabaster votive from the isolated Myrat complex. Wear and residue establish that its empty face is intentional and integral to the seal, not unfinished work or later damage.
provenance.function: required the maker or breaker to supply one complete pattern for its deliberate hollow.

provenance.summary: A Myrat teaching tablet pairs a flat two-mark source with the same open pattern already enclosed by an owner ring.
provenance.function: Demonstrated that constructing the exact owner boundary can create the missing ancestor-shaped source.

provenance.summary: Successive local owners place similar P and Q occurrences in hosts with different edit authority.
provenance.function: Distinguished legal edits by actual semantic host instead of visual proximity.

provenance.summary: A compound source, a descendant local owner, and a parallel workspace expose different copy-authority relations.
provenance.function: Required the intact compound to be copied only where its ownership ancestry grants authority.

provenance.summary: Neighboring atoms with different local owners make an otherwise atomic wrapping choice ownership-sensitive.
provenance.function: Required selecting the exact locally owned atom before introducing the paired boundary.

provenance.summary: A product result exposes one locally owned component as the exact input to a later implication.
provenance.function: Trained extraction and continuation while preserving each occurrence’s local owner.

provenance.summary: Two completed records and two locally scoped occurrences require exact whole-pattern selection.
provenance.function: Introduced selection of the exact completed artifact over a similar scoped pattern.

provenance.summary: Two related excluded-middle records are available beside a continuation shaped for a proper component of the three-owner record.
provenance.function: The authored route manifests the complete three-owner artifact, transforms its proper subcomponent, and uses that derived source downstream; ordinary construction leaves other valid proofs open.

provenance.summary: One completed artifact has several polarity-legal hosts but only one useful downstream placement.
provenance.function: Distinguished legal manifestation from strategically useful manifestation.

provenance.summary: A completed record has one exact ordinary occurrence inside a locally scoped identity context.
provenance.function: Introduced dissolution of an exact completed-record occurrence.

provenance.summary: An exact compound occurrence sits beside a contextually related but nonidentical scoped occurrence.
provenance.function: Required dissolution of the entire exact theorem side with no adjacent context.

provenance.summary: Manifested ordinary content becomes the exact ancestor source for a later structural copy.
provenance.function: Connected artifact manifestation to ordinary ancestry-based copy authority.

provenance.summary: One completed projection artifact has a negative manifestation host and a separate exact positive occurrence.
provenance.function: Use a proper component derived from forward manifestation, then reverse-dissolve the distinct exact occurrence.

provenance.summary: An exact dissolvable occurrence is also the sole ordinary authority for a later structural step.
provenance.function: Required preserving an artifact occurrence until its dependent copy no longer needs it.

provenance.summary: Related completed composition records compete to supply one exact downstream proposition.
provenance.function: Required the whole artifact whose manifested content exactly matches the consumer.
